observed_failure.surface: SkillGuard v0.3.2 candidate GitHub Actions full-current-suite on a clean Windows checkout
observed_failure.prior_local_claim: the generated contract and manifest were current after local compilation
observed_failure.observed_result: the isolated standalone target check blocked after 474 tests had passed because Git's Windows checkout changed generated JSON line endings from LF to CRLF and the compiler compared raw bytes

classification.error_signature: compiled-contract.json and check-manifest.json report stale_generated_contract only after a clean CRLF checkout

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: source fingerprints already normalized text line endings, but the same compiler's generated-authority parity boundary still used raw-byte equality and therefore assigned two identities to the same canonical JSON text
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the release model had replayed a clean Windows checkout whose generated JSON files were projected with CRLF while their canonical compiler payload remained LF
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: generated text authority has one canonical text identity across supported checkout line endings; byte spelling alone cannot make it stale
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: contract-compiler-v2 compile generated contract and verify generated parity

generalized_repair.rule: Normalize only physical line-ending bytes before comparing generated canonical JSON authority, while preserving all JSON content, keys, values, and ordering as authoritative.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: both generated authority files remain current after LF is projected as CRLF
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: LF, CRLF, and lone-CR physical spellings map to the same canonical text identity
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: a real generated JSON content change still produces stale_generated_contract; no semantic fallback, alternate reader, or weakened contract is introduced

required_revalidation: FlowGuard executable contract and validation-composition models
compiler line-ending positive and semantic-change negative regressions
standalone external-target replay with an explicitly empty CODEX_HOME
contract compiler and external binding modules
deterministic contract regeneration and check mode
clean Windows and Ubuntu GitHub Actions before v0.3.2 release

revalidation_evidence.model: both current executable model entrypoints pass with the raw-byte authority case rejected as known bad
revalidation_evidence.focused_regressions: 2 passed: generated authority checkout line-ending identity and standalone empty-install replay
revalidation_evidence.affected_modules: 59 tests and 7 subtests passed across the complete contract compiler and external target binding modules
revalidation_evidence.full_local_snapshot: 474 tests passed, 2 capability-dependent cases skipped, and 146 subtests passed on the frozen candidate source
revalidation_evidence.contract_compilation: contract CBD0CADE7889AA02DCC1F472F47A759F9076F80659F10B667741EF79AC269A8D and manifest 1497B91BA48E75C9BFA212308AEBD1D2F6430904E26DE83B1E73374F498741F2 compiled current with zero findings
revalidation_evidence.installed_parity: transaction install-3905228458294fc18388e4942ef6833d committed with all installed smoke checks passed; installation receipt installation-cace11889a23773c3b6f372c replayed current canonical-to-active parity
revalidation_evidence.remote_observation: GitHub Actions run 29481096934 passed all six focused Windows/Ubuntu jobs and failed only the clean full-current-suite at the generated-authority parity boundary

claim_boundary: The repair proves the named local model, focused, affected-module, and complete frozen-source regressions, deterministic contract compilation, transactional installation, and installed parity. A new clean GitHub run, tag, and GitHub Release remain pending claims.
